tool_args.label: MTA 2025 Final Proposed Budget, November Financial Plan 2025-2028, Volume 1

payload.title: MTA 2025 Final Proposed Budget
payload.description: November Financial Plan 2025 – 2028, Volume 1. Volume 1 consists of financial schedules supporting the complete MTA Consolidated Financial Plan, including an Executive Summary, the baseline forecast, and certain below-the-line adjustments.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Janno Lieber, Chair and Chief Executive Officer of MTA
tool_args.raw_text: I, Janno Lieber, Chair and Chief Executive Officer of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA") hereby certify... Dated: 11.15.24

tool_args.label: Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA)
tool_args.raw_text: MTA expects reimbursement of direct COVID-related expenses through the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) of $250 million in 2024, $140 million in 2025 and $210 million in 2026.

tool_args.label: Total Expenses before Non-Cash 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Total Expenses Before Non-Cash Liability Adjs. $17,355

tool_args.label: Fare/Toll Increase August 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Fare/Toll Increase, August 2025 (4% Yield) – An increase in fares and tolls, yielding a 4 percent overall increase in farebox and toll revenues, is assumed for implementation in August 2025

payload.description: An increase in fares and tolls, yielding a 4 percent overall increase in farebox and toll revenues, assumed for implementation in August 2025.

tool_args.label: Fare/Toll Increase March 2027
tool_args.raw_text: Fare/Toll Increase, March 2027 (4% Yield) – An increase in fares and tolls, yielding a 4% overall increase in farebox and toll revenues, is assumed for implementation in March 2027

payload.description: An increase in fares and tolls, yielding a 4% overall increase in farebox and toll revenues, assumed for implementation in March 2027.

tool_args.label: CBDTP toll revenue collection start
tool_args.raw_text: The Plan assumes collection of toll revenue from Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP) will commence in January 2025.

payload.event_name: Central Business District Tolling Program commencement
payload.description: The Plan assumes collection of toll revenue from Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP) will commence in January 2025.

tool_args.label: FEMA COVID Reimbursement 2024
tool_args.raw_text: MTA expects reimbursement of direct COVID-related expenses through the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) of $250 million in 2024

tool_args.label: November Plan balanced through 2026
tool_args.raw_text: The November Plan remains balanced through 2026 with deficits of $378 million in 2027 and $419 million in 2028

payload.claim_text: The November Plan remains balanced through 2026 with deficits of $378 million in 2027 and $419 million in 2028.
payload.subject: November Financial Plan

tool_args.label: Changes from July Plan favorable $100M
tool_args.raw_text: Changes from the July Plan are $100 million favorable over the Plan period

payload.claim_text: Changes from the July Plan are $100 million favorable over the Plan period
payload.subject: November Financial Plan vs July Plan

tool_args.label: Risk: fare evasion impact
tool_args.raw_text: Progress in reducing fare evasion is critical to balancing the financial plan. The potential cost for 5 percent lower recovery is estimated at $325 million per year.

payload.claim_text: Progress in reducing fare evasion is critical to balancing the financial plan. The potential cost for 5 percent lower recovery is estimated at $325 million per year.
payload.subject: MTA financial plan risks

tool_args.label: Risk: casino license uncertainty
tool_args.raw_text: The approval, awarding, and commencement of operations of downstate casinos is uncertain in both outcome and timing, which risks the $500 million assumed to be received by MTA in 2026 and 2027, and the $600 million assumed for 2028.

payload.claim_text: The approval, awarding, and commencement of operations of downstate casinos is uncertain in both outcome and timing, which risks the $500 million assumed to be received by MTA in 2026 and 2027, and the $600 million assumed for 2028.
payload.subject: MTA financial plan risks

tool_args.label: Risk: Paratransit reimbursement
tool_args.raw_text: The financial plan assumes the extension of legislation requiring the city of New York to fund 80% of the net paratransit operating expenses. The potential impact of reverting to 50% reimbursement is estimated at $200 million growing to $250 million per year.

payload.claim_text: The financial plan assumes the extension of legislation requiring the city of New York to fund 80% of the net paratransit operating expenses. The potential impact of reverting to 50% reimbursement is estimated at $200 million growing to $250 million per year.
payload.subject: MTA financial plan risks

tool_args.label: Risk: Capital Program funding
tool_args.raw_text: Approval and funding for 2025-2029 Capital Program. Funding for the next five-year capital program is needed for MTA to continue its investment in critical state of good repair projects for safe and reliable service. Securing substantial new federal, state and city funding will be required.

payload.claim_text: Funding for the next five-year capital program is needed for MTA to continue its investment in critical state of good repair projects for safe and reliable service. Securing substantial new federal, state and city funding will be required.
payload.subject: 2025-2029 Capital Program

payload.context: By MTA Agency after below-the-line adjustments

tool_args.label: Operating Efficiencies savings target
tool_args.raw_text: The February Plan baseline included operating efficiencies initiatives that are expected to generate $1.88 billion through 2027 directly impacting MTA. The November Plan includes an additional $312 million in savings, which have been incorporated into Agency baseline financial plans and meet the MTA annual $500 million savings target from Operating Efficiencies as of 2025.

payload.claim_text: The February Plan baseline included operating efficiencies initiatives that are expected to generate $1.88 billion through 2027 directly impacting MTA. The November Plan includes an additional $312 million in savings, which have been incorporated into Agency baseline financial plans and meet the MTA annual $500 million savings target from Operating Efficiencies as of 2025.
payload.subject: MTA Operating Efficiencies
